text: Hi guys, this is Dr. Andrew Iraniha from Surgical Oasis Institute. Here I'm going to show you on this video my technique for robotic ventral hernia repair with mesh.

This is a forty five year old gentleman who presented initially with a large bulge in upper abdomen and found to have a large hernia he was taken to the operating room and I performed this operation with the robotic technology here you can see the actual defect in the mid abdomen and initially after placement of the special ports and special instruments I go back into the

The surgeon uses robotic technology to close the abdominal defect, incorporating part of the hernia sac to eliminate the space between the skin and abdominal muscle. Strong, heavy sutures are used to close the defect in two layers, with reduced intra-abdominal pressure to minimize tension on the wound.
The surgeon places a mesh over the abdominal defect to reinforce the closure, addressing the significant tension due to the patient's obesity.

The surgeon inserts a two-layered mesh into the abdomen. The rough layer is positioned to attach to the abdominal wall, while the smoother hydrogel layer, which is absorbable, faces the intra-abdominal structures.

The surgeon uses special suturing techniques to attach the mesh securely to the abdominal wall, ensuring no intra-abdominal structures can become trapped between the mesh and the abdominal wall. Over two weeks, the abdominal wall incorporates the mesh into the musculature, forming a sheet over the mesh.
That usually takes around two weeks and that's enough to prevent the future scar tissue to the mesh.

The surgeon carefully sutures the mesh to the abdominal wall, ensuring the rough side faces the wall and not the intra-abdominal structures. A ring in the middle of the mesh is visible, which will be removed later. The surgeon uses precise suturing techniques to avoid wrinkling the mesh while ensuring it is securely attached without excessive tension.

So minimally invasive surgery including robotic and laparoscopic operation allows the surgeon to perform this operation with minimal trauma to the abdominal wall. Compared to the open technique when we open the abdominal wall and close the hole and place the mesh on the top of the muscle, This operation is much less painful and the recovery is much, much faster.
The surgeon removes the ring from the mesh through the port, showing it completely separated from the abdominal wall.

The surgeon places additional sutures in the middle of the mesh, securing it to the abdominal wall. The mesh is visibly attached without tension or wrinkles, ensuring it is completely secured to the abdominal wall muscle.

This patient did very well after the surgery, went home, stayed one hour in the recovery and for a couple days had some abdominal discomfort requiring pain medication but then went back to work after a week with abdominal binder and six weeks after the surgery started heavy exercise without any problem.
If you like this video, please follow my YouTube channel with the name of Surgical Oasis Institute or call me if you have any question at nine four nine six four six eight four four four. Thank you.
